findings.description: Cross-reference to Section 6 is broken. The text states 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6' but no Section 6 exists anywhere in the document.
findings.evidence: Stage 4 — Monitoring: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' The document ends after the Conclusions section with no Section 6.
findings.suggestion: Either add a Section 6 covering security considerations, or replace the reference with inline security content, or remove the sentence and note security is out of scope for this document.

findings.description: Direct contradiction about pipeline topology. The Overview states all stages run in parallel; Stage 2 states Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3.
findings.evidence: Overview: 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' Stage 2: 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins.'
findings.suggestion: Correct the Overview to accurately describe the pipeline topology. For a standard ETL pipeline, stages are sequential; remove or qualify the 'all stages run in parallel' claim.

findings.description: The claim that the in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% is implausible and unsupported. An in-memory write buffer does not reduce end-to-end pipeline latency by 99.9%.
findings.evidence: Stage 3: 'The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes.'
findings.suggestion: Replace with a defensible, qualified claim (e.g., 'significantly reduces write latency' or cite an actual benchmark with methodology).

findings.description: Authentication method is completely unspecified. A final architecture document must identify what authentication mechanism is used.
findings.evidence: Stage 1: 'Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.' No method, protocol, token type, or header specified.
findings.suggestion: Specify the authentication mechanism (e.g., 'API key authentication via the Authorization header', 'OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens', etc.).

findings.description: 'Standard retry policy' is referenced without definition. A final architecture document should define the retry policy or explicitly reference where it is defined.
findings.evidence: Stage 2: 'Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures.' No policy parameters (max retries, backoff, delay) are defined anywhere in the document.
findings.suggestion: Define the retry policy inline (e.g., 'exponential backoff with up to 3 retries, starting at 1 second') or explicitly reference the document where it is defined.

findings.description: The Conclusions section's scalability claim covers only 3 of 4 pipeline stages, silently omitting Stage 4 (Monitoring).
findings.evidence: Conclusions: 'The staged architecture enables independent scaling of ingestion, transformation, and delivery based on load patterns.' Stage 4 — Monitoring is absent.
findings.suggestion: Update to include all four stages, or explicitly note that Monitoring scales differently.

findings.description: A specific benchmark result (3× faster) is cited without source, methodology, or test conditions.
findings.evidence: Performance Characteristics: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.'
findings.suggestion: Add benchmark source, test conditions, and methodology, or qualify as 'internal benchmarks' with conditions noted.

findings.description: Authentication and request verification are conflated. Authentication establishes caller identity; schema validation (which the document already describes separately) is what verifies request content.
findings.evidence: Stage 1: 'Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.' The document already describes schema validation ('validated against a JSON schema on arrival') separately.
findings.suggestion: Clarify: 'Requests are authenticated before processing begins.' (Separate from schema validation already described.)

findings.description: The overflow queue is mentioned without defining what threshold or condition triggers overflow routing.
findings.evidence: Stage 2: 'Large files are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.' No size threshold is defined.
findings.suggestion: Define the threshold (e.g., 'Files exceeding 10MB are routed to the overflow queue') or reference where the threshold is configured.
